_doc: Durable registry of problematic catalog pages found during extraction. Authored by the assistant when a page can't be extracted cleanly/confidently; surfaced in the taxonomy UI under 'Other > Needs Review' for human review. status: open = needs review/decision; resolved = fixed (see resolution). This file is the shared record both the user and the assistant refer back to.

issues.section: GRASS TIOMOS SOFT-CLOSE / SELF-CLOSE EURO HINGES
issues.problem: Pages 48 & 52 ('TIOMOS 95° Hinges for Thick Doors') use variant column headers 'Closing Type'/'Fixing Type' instead of the 'Close Type'/'Fixing' used on the other TIOMOS pages. The first extractor gated on the exact 'Close Type' header and silently dropped all 32 hinges on these two pages — caught only by the page-text-layer cross-check.
issues.resolution: Re-gated the TIOMOS extractor on the 'Boring Pattern' column and read cells by header substring; also pulls the 95° opening angle from these titles. Re-verified vs the page text layer: 116 TIOMOS hinges, 0 phantom. (commit fd8ae49)

issues.section: GRASS TEC SOFT-CLOSE / SELF-CLOSE / FACE-MOUNT HINGES
issues.problem: TEC hinge pages are two side-by-side tables in one block (e.g. Side-Mount 45mm | Side-Mount 42mm). The cell parser keys cells by header name, so the duplicated columns collide and the right-column SKUs are silently lost: B-85 had 25 hinge SKUs in the page text but only 7 in the parsed cells (18 lost); ~56 lost across the 5 pages. Counts alone looked fine — only the page-text cross-check exposed it.
issues.resolution: Wrote a positional TEC extractor (extract_grass_tec) that reads SKUs from the word layer within each block's column x-bands instead of by header name, binding overlay/fixing/mount/closing and the section-level opening angle. Re-verified vs page text: all 84 TEC hinges, 0 missed / 0 phantom / 0 dup on all 7 pages; both columns confirmed boxed on the render.
